title: Flamingo: a Visual Language Model for Few-Shot Learning
hook: Flamingo learns from just a few examples by blending vision and language models.
promise: You will understand how Flamingo links frozen vision and language models for flexible, few-shot multimodal learning.
prerequisite_note: Bring your curiosity about how AI can connect images and text, no deep math required.

glossary.plain: A neural network whose weights are kept fixed during training on a new task.
Training a model to perform a new task with only a handful of examples.
Combining information from different types of data, such as images and text.
A neural network model that processes images using attention mechanisms.
A neural network operation that allows information to flow between different data types, like images and text.
A model that processes both images and language to generate or understand text about images.

cards.title: Images meet words, few-shot style
body: Learn how Flamingo connects vision and language models for powerful few-shot multimodal learning.

cards.title: Can AI answer questions about any image?
body: Imagine showing an AI a handful of image-text pairs and expecting it to answer questions about new pictures.
Flamingo makes this possible by linking frozen vision and language models, letting the system learn new tasks with just a few examples.

cards.title: How Flamingo flows from vision to language
body: Flamingo starts with powerful vision and language models, each trained separately and kept frozen.
It adds a small, trainable connector that lets these models talk, so the system can learn from a few new examples.
Frozen vision and language models provide strong starting points.
A cross-attention connector lets language query visual features.
Few-shot multimodal learning makes new image tasks promptable.
Flamingo’s architecture enables rapid adaptation to new tasks with minimal extra training.
cards.caption: Flamingo’s architecture enables rapid adaptation to new tasks with minimal extra training.

cards.title: How few-shot multimodal learning changes the game
body: Compare how old models and Flamingo handle new image-text tasks with few examples.
cards.offNote: Requires retraining the whole model for each new task, which is slow and data-hungry.
cards.onNote: Keeps vision and language models frozen, learning new tasks from just a few examples with a small connector.

cards.title: The clever connector
body: Flamingo’s key trick is to keep both vision and language models frozen, adding a cross-attention layer between them.
This small connector allows the combined model to quickly adapt to new multimodal tasks without retraining the large components.

cards.title: How many examples does Flamingo need?
body: Adjust the slider to see how Flamingo performs with more or fewer examples for each new task.
cards.low: Performance is limited, but Flamingo still outperforms older models on new tasks.
cards.mid: With a handful of examples, Flamingo adapts well and answers accurately.
cards.high: With more examples, Flamingo approaches the performance of fully supervised models.

cards.title: Pick Flamingo’s core ingredients
body: Select the six critical parts that make Flamingo a few-shot multimodal learner.

choices.label: Frozen Vision Transformer
choices.note: Extracts features from images without further training.

choices.label: Frozen Language Model
choices.note: Generates or understands text, weights kept fixed.

choices.label: Cross-Attention Layers
choices.note: Link vision and language, letting them share information.

choices.label: Multimodal Prompting
choices.note: Feeds both images and text as examples to the model.

choices.label: Small Trainable Connector
choices.note: Learns to bridge the vision and language models.

choices.label: Few-Shot Task Examples
choices.note: Provides a handful of demonstrations for new tasks.

cards.title: Flamingo’s few-shot performance across tasks
body: Flamingo set new benchmarks on a range of multimodal tasks, including visual question answering and image captioning.
Its few-shot learning ability allowed it to outperform previous models with far less data and training.
cards.caption: Flamingo’s architecture leads to strong results on a wide range of multimodal benchmarks with minimal examples.

cards.title: Why Flamingo is a landmark for multimodal AI
body: Flamingo showed that you can connect powerful vision and language models without retraining them, unlocking rapid few-shot learning.
This approach paved the way for new research into flexible, efficient multimodal systems that learn from just a few examples.

recap.label: Combine
recap.note: Use frozen vision and language models.

recap.label: Connect
recap.note: Add cross-attention layers as a bridge.

cards.title: Flamingo: a Visual Language Model for Few-Shot Learning
body: Read the full paper for technical details and experimental results.
